info.title: mboek API
info.description:
REST API for the mboek (bookkeeping) application.

**Authentication**: All endpoints except `/api/auth/login` require a Bearer JWT token. Obtain a token via `POST /api/auth/login` and pass it in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.

**Known inconsistencies**:
- Boekingen are listed/created under `/api/dagboeken/{id}/boekingen` but read/updated/deleted at `/api/boekingen/{id}`
- `/api/vacuum` has no admin-only guard
- `/api/administraties/import` must be registered before `/{id}` to avoid path collision

get.description: Returns all administraties (company administrations) owned by the authenticated user, sorted alphabetically by name. Each user sees only their own administraties.

200.description: List of administraties owned by the current user

post.description: Creates a new administratie owned by the authenticated user. The name is required; KvK number, BTW number, address, and description are optional.

Imports a previously exported administratie JSON file (type=administratie). Creates a new administratie owned by the authenticated user with all its data (grootboekrekeningen, dagboeken, BTW codes, auto-booking rules, boekjaren, boekingen). If `overwrite=true` and an administratie with the same name already exists, it will be replaced. The entire import is done in a single transaction.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: If true, overwrite existing administratie with same name

schema.description: Dummy schemas for export-import response bodies.

422.description: Validation error (wrong type or missing fields)

post.description: Applies all active auto-booking rules to a single boeking. If a rule matches, the boeking's staging-account regel (9990) is replaced with the categorized account lines defined by the matching rule. Only the first matching rule (by priority) is applied. Returns `{"matched": true}` if a rule fired, `{"matched": false}` otherwise.

schema.description: Dummy schema type for the apply-to-boeking response.

get.description: Exports a single boeking and all its regels as a JSON file download. Useful for inspecting a specific entry or copying it to another dagboek.

200.description: Single boeking export as JSON file download

get.description: Returns all fiscal years (boekjaren) for an administratie, ordered by start date. Status is either `open` (accepting new boekingen) or `gesloten` (closed).

post.description: Creates a new fiscal year for an administratie. New boekjaren start with status `open`. Provide `naam`, `start_datum` (YYYY-MM-DD), and `eind_datum` (YYYY-MM-DD).

Imports a previously exported boekjaar JSON file (type=boekjaar) into an existing administratie. All references in the export (dagboek codes, grootboekrekening codes, BTW codes) must exist in the target administratie. Returns 409 if a boekjaar with the same name already exists. The entire import is transactional.

422.description: Validation error (wrong type or missing references)

get.description: Returns the details of a single boekjaar.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a boekjaar and all its boekingen. This action is irreversible.

patch.description: Updates the name, dates, or other fields of a boekjaar. To change the status, use the dedicated `afsluiten` or `heropenen` endpoints instead.

post.description: Atomically transitions a boekjaar from status `open` to `gesloten`. A closed boekjaar can no longer receive new boekingen. This is a prerequisite for finalising BTW aangiften (`vastleggen`). Returns 409 if the boekjaar is already gesloten.

get.description: Exports a single boekjaar with all its boekingen and BTW aangiften as a JSON file download. The export includes references by code (dagboek, grootboekrekening, BTW code) so it can be imported into an administratie with matching codes.

200.description: Boekjaar export as JSON file download

post.description: Reopens a gesloten boekjaar by setting its status back to `open`. Blocked with 409 if the boekjaar has any definitieve BTW aangiften, as reopening would invalidate locked VAT returns.

409.description: Conflict — not closed or has definitieve BTW aangiften

post.description: Sets the `huidig_boekjaar_id` field on the administratie to this boekjaar. This value is used as the default boekjaar in queries that accept an optional `boekjaar_id`, such as the dagboeken `werkstatus` endpoint.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Boekjaar ID to set as current

get.description: Returns the BTW (VAT) return status for all 4 quarters of a boekjaar. Quarters that have not yet been calculated are included as synthetic entries with status `niet_berekend` and zero amounts, so the response always contains 4 items. Calculated quarters have status `concept`; finalised quarters have status `definitief`.

200.description: List of BTW aangiften (all 4 quarters synthesized if not yet calculated)

post.description: Calculates (or recalculates) the VAT return for a specific quarter. Aggregates all BTW-coded boekingsregels in the quarter's date range and maps them to the standard Dutch VAT return fields (1a through 5g). Creates a new record or updates an existing one; result has status `concept`. Returns 409 if the aangifte is already `definitief` (use delete + recalculate instead).

200.description: BTW aangifte berekening (created or updated as concept)

delete.description: Deletes a BTW aangifte record. Only concept-status aangiften can be deleted. Returns 409 if the aangifte is definitief.

schema.description: Dummy schema for delete response `{"deleted": N}`.

post.description: Locks a concept BTW aangifte as definitief (final). Requires the boekjaar to be gesloten first — you cannot finalise a VAT return for an open fiscal year. Once definitief, the aangifte cannot be recalculated and the boekjaar cannot be heropened.

409.description: Boekjaar must be gesloten before vastleggen

get.description: Returns all BTW (VAT) codes for an administratie, sorted by code. Each code defines a VAT rate, type, and optionally the linked output/input rekeningen.

post.description: Creates a new BTW code. `percentage` is a decimal percentage (e.g. 21.0 for 21%). `pct_aftrek` (deductibility percentage) defaults to 100% when omitted. Optionally link `output_rekening_id` (BTW te betalen) and `input_rekening_id` (BTW te vorderen). New codes are created as inactive (`actief = false`) — activate them when appropriate for your business.

Creates the standard Dutch BTW codes (V21, V9, V0, I21, I9, EU21, INT21, VRL) if they do not already exist. Skips any code that is already present. Output/input rekeningen are auto-resolved by looking up grootboekrekeningen with codes 2310 (Te betalen BTW) and 2320 (Te vorderen BTW) if they exist. All seeded codes are created as inactive (`actief = false`).

204.description: Default BTW codes seeded (skips existing codes)

get.description: Returns a single BTW code by ID.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a BTW code. Note: deleting a code that is referenced by existing boekingsregels may cause referential issues.

patch.description: Partially updates a BTW code. All request body fields are optional; omit a field to leave it unchanged.

get.description: Returns all dagboeken (journals/sub-ledgers) for an administratie, sorted by code. Dagboek types include: `bank`, `kas`, `inkoop`, `verkoop`, and `memoriaal`.

post.description: Creates a new dagboek. For bank-type dagboeken, set `grootboekrekening_id` to the linked bank account in the chart of accounts, and optionally set `iban` for IBAN-based matching during bank statement import.

get.description: Returns per-dagboek work-status counts for a given boekjaar: `onverwerkt` counts boekingen that still have a line pointing to the bankimport staging account (9990, i.e. unprocessed bank imports), and `te_bevestigen` counts boekingen that were auto-booked by rules but not yet manually confirmed. Defaults to the administratie's `huidig_boekjaar_id` when `boekjaar_id` is omitted.

parameters.name: boekjaar_id
parameters.description: Boekjaar ID (defaults to huidig_boekjaar_id of the administratie)

post.description: Imports previously exported boekingen into a specific dagboek of an administratie. The target boekjaar must be open. All referenced grootboekrekening codes and BTW codes must exist in the target administratie. Boekingen are inserted atomically.

requestBody.description: JSON object with 'boekingen' array of ExportBoeking

422.description: Validation error (missing references or gesloten boekjaar)

post.summary: Re-run auto-booking rules on all unbooked entries (contra = bankimport rekening) for a dagboek
post.description: Re-applies all active auto-booking rules to all currently-unprocessed boekingen in the dagboek across all open boekjaren. A boeking is considered unprocessed when it still has a regel pointing to the bankimport staging account (9990). For each matching boeking, the staging-account regel is replaced with categorized account lines. Returns the count of boekingen that were updated.

schema.description: Dummy schema type for the rerun/apply response `{"updated": N}`.

post.description: Suggests contra-rekeningen (counter accounts) for a bank transaction based on its description and optional tegenpartij_naam. Looks up previously used contra-accounts for similar transactions in the same dagboek and ranks them by frequency of use. Useful for presenting booking suggestions to the user before they confirm an import.

get.description: Returns the details of a single dagboek, including its linked grootboekrekening and IBAN if configured.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a dagboek. This will fail if the dagboek has existing boekingen (foreign key constraint).

patch.description: Partially updates a dagboek. All fields are optional; omit a field to leave it unchanged.

get.description: Exports the complete administratie as a JSON file download. The export includes all BTW codes, grootboekrekeningen, dagboeken, auto-booking rules, boekjaren, and all boekingen with their regels. Suitable for full backup or migration to another instance. The exported file can be re-imported with the import endpoint.

200.description: Full administratie export as JSON file download

get.description: Returns all grootboekrekeningen (chart of accounts entries) for an administratie, sorted by code. Includes all types: activa, passiva, kosten, opbrengsten, and bijzonder.

post.description: Creates a new grootboekrekening. `rekening_type` must be one of: `activa`, `passiva`, `kosten`, `opbrengsten`, `bijzonder`. Optionally set `parent_id` to nest under another rekening and `rgs_code` for Dutch RGS (Referentie Grootboekschema) compatibility.

get.description: Returns all grootboekrekeningen for an administratie, enriched with the number of boekingsregels (`aantal_transacties`) and the net balance (`saldo`, in euro cents, positive = debet) for the given boekjaar. Accounts with no transactions in the boekjaar are included with zeros. Useful for scripts and for filtering the chart of accounts in the UI.

200.description: All grootboekrekeningen with transaction count and saldo

post.description: Seeds a standard Dutch RGS (Referentie Grootboekschema) chart of accounts with ~15 common account categories (1000–4900) if those codes do not already exist. Skips any code that is already present. Run `seed-defaults` on btw-codes afterwards to set up linked BTW rekeningen.

get.description: Returns a single grootboekrekening by ID.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a grootboekrekening. Will fail if the account is referenced by existing boekingsregels or BTW codes.

patch.description: Updates the fields of a grootboekrekening. All fields are optional. Use `actief = false` to deactivate an account without deleting it.

post.description: Imports a bank statement file (MT940 or CAMT.053 format) into the matching dagboeken. The format is auto-detected from the filename and file content. Transactions are matched to dagboeken by IBAN. Existing transactions are deduplicated by a SHA-256 hash of immutable transaction fields (own IBAN, date, signed amount, bank reference, description, counterparty IBAN) unless `allow_duplicates = true` is set in the form. A bankimport staging account (code 9990) is created automatically if it does not exist. After import, all active auto-booking rules are applied to the new boekingen. Returns counts of imported, skipped (duplicates, zero-amount), and auto-booked transactions.

requestBody.description: MT940 or CAMT.053 bank statement file

schema.description:
Form data for bank statement upload. Send `file` as a file field and
optionally `allow_duplicates=true` to re-import transactions already present.

allow_duplicates.description: Set to "true" to re-import transactions already present (default: false)

file.description: Bank statement file (MT940 or CAMT.053 XML)

get.description: Generates a balance sheet (balans) for a boekjaar. Aggregates all boekingsregels for active activa and passiva grootboekrekeningen. Returns per-account debet/credit/saldo values and totals. `in_balans` is true when the absolute difference between activa and passiva totals is less than €0.01.

Generates a profit & loss report (winst & verlies) for a boekjaar. Aggregates all boekingsregels for active kosten, opbrengsten, and bijzonder grootboekrekeningen. Returns per-account amounts and the `netto_resultaat` (opbrengsten − kosten − bijzonder). Accounts with zero balance are omitted.

Returns all auto-booking rules for an administratie, each with their rule lines (match criteria and booking actions). Rules are ordered by priority (lower number = higher priority). Rules are matched against imported bank transaction descriptions to automatically categorize boekingen.

200.description: List of auto-booking rules with lines

post.description: Creates a new auto-booking rule with optional rule lines. A rule can match by: own IBAN (`iban_eigen`), counterparty IBAN (`iban_tegenpartij`), and/or description regex (`omschrijving_regex`). Each rule line defines an account and optional BTW code to book to when the rule fires. Lower `prioriteit` values take precedence. Set `actief = false` to create a disabled rule.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an auto-booking rule and all its lines.

patch.description: Partially updates an auto-booking rule's fields. If `lines` is provided, the existing rule lines are replaced atomically. All fields are optional; omit a field to leave it unchanged.

get.description: Returns the full mutation ledger for a single grootboekrekening within a boekjaar: every boekingsregel with its date, dagboek code/name, journal entry description, line description, and amount (positive = debet, negative = credit). Ordered by date and id.

200.description: List of mutations for this rekening in the given boekjaar

get.description: Returns the full details of a single administratie. Returns 403 if the authenticated user is not the owner.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an administratie and all associated data (boekjaren, dagboeken, boekingen, BTW codes, grootboekrekeningen, etc.). This action is irreversible. Only the owner may delete it.

patch.description:
Partially updates an administratie. All request body fields are optional; omit a field to leave it unchanged.
        To change the active boekjaar shown by default in queries, set `huidig_boekjaar_id`.

post.description: Authenticates a user with username and password. Returns a signed JWT bearer token. Argon2id password hashing is always run regardless of whether the user exists, preventing username enumeration via timing attacks.
Revokes the current JWT token server-side. The token remains syntactically valid but is rejected by all protected endpoints until it expires naturally.

get.description: Returns the username and subject claim from the current bearer token. Useful for verifying a token is still valid and retrieving the logged-in username.
Returns a single boeking with all its boekingsregels. Unlike the list endpoint (under a dagboek), this endpoint is routed directly under `/api/boekingen/{id}` for convenient single-record access.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a boeking and all its boekingsregels in a transaction.

patch.description: Updates a boeking's fields and optionally replaces all its boekingsregels. If `regels` is provided, the existing regels are deleted and the new set is inserted atomically. Manually editing regels automatically clears `auto_geboekt` and `gecontroleerd` flags.

get.description: Returns all boekingen (journal entries) for a dagboek within a boekjaar, each including their boekingsregels (lines). Ordered by date and id. The `boekjaar_id` query parameter is required.

post.description: Creates a new boeking with its boekingsregels in a single transaction. All regels must balance (sum of bedragen must equal zero). Set `auto_geboekt = true` to flag the boeking as system-generated (it will then appear in the `te_bevestigen` work-status count until manually confirmed).

Runs SQLite VACUUM to reclaim disk space and defragment the database. Note: this endpoint is only protected by JWT authentication — there is no additional admin guard. Any authenticated user can trigger a VACUUM.

CreateAdministratieInput.description: API input for creating an administratie

DagboekWerkStatus.description: Per-dagboek work-status counts for the current boekjaar.

onverwerkt.description: Boekingen that still use the bankimport holding account (need manual booking).

te_bevestigen.description: Auto-booked entries that need human confirmation.

aantal_transacties.description: Number of boekingsregels in the given boekjaar.

saldo.description: Net balance in euro cents (positive = debet, negative = credit).

parse_warnings.description: Parse warnings from the bank statement file (e.g. malformed transaction lines).

ImportUploadForm.description:
Form data for bank statement upload. Send `file` as a file field and
optionally `allow_duplicates=true` to re-import transactions already present.

allow_duplicates.description: Set to "true" to re-import transactions already present (default: false)

file.description: Bank statement file (MT940 or CAMT.053 XML)

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp when this token expires.

omschrijving.description: Transaction description to match against prior boekingen

UpdateAdministratieInput.description: API input for updating an administratie

UpdateAutoBookingRuleInput.description:
Partial-update input for PATCH /regels/:id.
All fields are optional; absent fields leave the current value unchanged.
Nullable fields use `Option<Option<T>>` so that explicit `null` in JSON
clears the field, while omission leaves it untouched.
